steps.title: Extract the center and radius of Circle A
steps.content: The equation $(x-7)^2 + (y+3)^2 = 1$ is in standard form $(x-h)^2 + (y-k)^2 = r^2$, where the center is $(h, k)$ and radius is $r$. Comparing, Circle A has center $(7, -3)$ and radius \$1$.

steps.title: Apply the translation right 4 units
steps.content: Translating right means adding 4 to the x-coordinate of the center. The new center is $(7 + 4, -3) = (11, -3)$. The radius remains \$1$ since translations don't change size. The equation of Circle B is $(x-11)^2 + (y+3)^2 = 1$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
